name: widescreen-ui
desc: Legacy diagnostic uniform-scale patch, not the production remaster path. The 2D UI stretches at a non-4:3 backbuffer because FUN_004ea460 @0x004ea460 computes TWO INDEPENDENT per-axis scale ratios after GetClientRect: _DAT_00772e2c = virtualW(this+0x2a3d8)/clientW and _DAT_00772e30 = virtualH(this+0x2a3dc)/clientH. This patch forces both globals to a single UNIFORM factor by copying X into Y. It preserves aspect but still treats 4:3-authored UI as an anchored legacy island. Current player-facing remaster must use native scene coordinates, e.g. lobby-res + lobby-native-layout, not this patch.
verified: BYTE PATCH RESOLVED (2026-06-19), but superseded for the player-facing remaster on 2026-06-20. Byte-level disasm of FUN_004ea460 captured (capstone over .omo/ghidra/bin/G7MTClient.exe, fileoff 0xea460). The two stores are: X `fstp [0x772e2c]` @0x004ea4dd (d91d2c2e7700, = virtualW/clientW) and Y `fstp [0x772e30]` @0x004ea4ff (d91d302e7700, = virtualH/clientH). Consumers byte-PROVEN: FUN_004ea610 @0x004ea610 applies `fmul [0x772e2c]` to left/right and `fmul [0x772e30]` to top/bottom. Keep this descriptor for stretch isolation only; default builds intentionally omit it.

patches.note: Y-region uniform-scale (copy X into Y). Original (34B): mov ecx,[esi+0x2a3dc]; mov [esp+0x10],ecx; fild qword [esp+0x10]; pop esi; pop ebx; fstp [esp+0x1c]; fld [esp+0x1c]; fdiv [esp+4]; fstp [0x772e30] (= virtualH/clientH). Patched (34B, same length): pop esi; pop ebx; mov eax,[0x772e2c]; mov [0x772e30],eax; nop*22 (=> _DAT_00772e30 := _DAT_00772e2c = min ratio for 16:9-fill). pop esi/pop ebx kept first so stack stays balanced; x87 stack already empty so no fpu imbalance. The X store @0x004ea4dd is left untouched.

todo: DONE: Disassembled FUN_004ea460 @0x004ea460 byte-level; store sites X@0x004ea4dd (d91d2c2e7700) Y@0x004ea4ff (d91d302e7700); consumers FUN_004ea610 fmul-byte-proven.
DONE: Encoded in-place 34-byte copy-X-into-Y overwrite (same length, x87/stack balanced). patches[] populated.
Do not wire into the default playable stack. Use only as an A/B diagnostic when isolating scaler stretch from native anchor-table bugs.
For production remaster work, patch per-scene anchor tables and assets for the native canvas instead of preserving a 4:3 UI island.

OPTIONAL (robustness): a true min(X,Y) variant (covers taller-than-4:3 clients where Y<X) does NOT fit inline because esi(=this) must be read for virtualH before the mandatory `pop esi`; it would need a code cave (detour from the Y region to spare .text padding, compute min via fld+fcom/fcmov, store into both globals). The copy-X patch is exact-min for the 16:9-fill target and is preferred unless taller-than-4:3 is a target.
preconditions: Diagnostic only: GraphicConfig.txt ScreenWidth/ScreenHeight set to a non-4:3 resolution.
dgVoodoo present (exe/D3D8.dll) advertising the arbitrary fullscreen mode.
